audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Mahanaim, Esau messengers, Jacob's fear/prayer, gift strategy, night crossing, wrestling figure, Israel name, Peniel, and limp/hip tradition.

verses.restored: Jacob went on his way, and messengers of the Divine met him.

familiar.text: And Jacob went on his way, and the angels of God met him.

verses.restored: When Jacob saw them, he said, This is the camp of the Divine. He called the name of that place Mahanaim.

familiar.text: And when Jacob saw them, he said, This is God's host...

notes.title: Mahanaim
notes.body: Mahanaim means two camps or camps, matching the divine-human camp motif.

verses.restored: Jacob sent messengers before him to Esau his brother, to the land of Seir, the field of Edom.

familiar.text: And Jacob sent messengers before him to Esau his brother...

verses.restored: He commanded them, saying, Thus you shall say to my lord Esau: Thus says your servant Jacob, I have sojourned with Laban and stayed until now.

familiar.text: And he commanded them, saying, Thus shall ye speak unto my lord Esau...

verses.restored: I have oxen, donkeys, flocks, male servants, and female servants, and I have sent to tell my lord, to find favor in your eyes.

familiar.text: And I have oxen, and asses, flocks, and menservants...

verses.restored: The messengers returned to Jacob, saying, We came to your brother Esau, and he is also coming to meet you, with four hundred men with him.

familiar.text: And the messengers returned to Jacob, saying, We came to thy brother Esau...

verses.restored: Jacob was very afraid and distressed. He divided the people who were with him, the flocks, herds, and camels, into two camps.

familiar.text: Then Jacob was greatly afraid and distressed...

verses.restored: He said, If Esau comes to the one camp and strikes it, then the remaining camp will escape.

familiar.text: And said, If Esau come to the one company, and smite it...

verses.restored: Jacob said, Divine of my father Abraham and Divine of my father Isaac, Eternal, who said to me, Return to your land and to your kindred, and I will do good with you,

familiar.text: And Jacob said, O God of my father Abraham, and God of my father Isaac...

notes.title: Prayer of return
notes.body: Jacob prays by recalling the return command and promise.

verses.restored: I am too small for all the covenant-kindnesses and all the truth that you have done with your servant. With my staff I crossed this Jordan, and now I have become two camps.

familiar.text: I am not worthy of the least of all the mercies...

notes.title: Too small
notes.body: Jacob confesses humility before chesed and emet, loyal kindness and truth.

verses.restored: Please rescue me from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau, for I fear him, lest he come and strike me, mother upon children.

familiar.text: Deliver me, I pray thee, from the hand of my brother...

verses.restored: But you said, I will surely do good with you and make your seed like the sand of the sea, which cannot be counted for multitude.

familiar.text: And thou saidst, I will surely do thee good...

verses.restored: He spent that night there, and from what came into his hand he took a gift for Esau his brother:

familiar.text: And he lodged there that same night; and took of that which came to his hand...

verses.restored: two hundred female goats and twenty male goats, two hundred ewes and twenty rams,

familiar.text: Two hundred she goats, and twenty he goats...

verses.restored: thirty nursing camels with their young, forty cows and ten bulls, twenty female donkeys and ten male donkeys.

familiar.text: Thirty milch camels with their colts, forty kine...

verses.restored: He gave them into the hand of his servants, drove by drove, and said to his servants, Pass over before me, and put space between drove and drove.

familiar.text: And he delivered them into the hand of his servants, every drove by themselves...

verses.restored: He commanded the first, saying, When Esau my brother meets you and asks you, saying, Whose are you? Where are you going? Whose are these before you?

familiar.text: And he commanded the foremost, saying, When Esau my brother meeteth thee...

verses.restored: then you shall say, They belong to your servant Jacob. It is a gift sent to my lord Esau, and look, he also is behind us.

familiar.text: Then thou shalt say, They be thy servant Jacob's...

verses.restored: He commanded the second and the third and all who followed the droves, saying, In this way you shall speak to Esau when you find him,

familiar.text: And so commanded he the second, and the third...

verses.restored: and you shall say, Also your servant Jacob is behind us. For he said, I will cover his face with the gift that goes before my face; afterward I will see his face. Perhaps he will lift my face.

familiar.text: And say ye moreover, Behold, thy servant Jacob is behind us...

notes.title: Face language
notes.body: Jacob’s gift strategy is full of face-language: cover face, see face, lift face. Reconciliation is embodied in the face.

verses.restored: So the gift passed over before his face, while he spent that night in the camp.

familiar.text: So went the present over before him: and himself lodged that night...

verses.restored: He rose that night, took his two women, his two slave-women, and his eleven children, and crossed the ford of Jabbok.

familiar.text: And he rose up that night, and took his two wives...

verses.restored: He took them and sent them across the stream, and sent across what belonged to him.

familiar.text: And he took them, and sent them over the brook...

verses.restored: Jacob was left alone, and a man wrestled with him until the rising of the dawn.

familiar.text: And Jacob was left alone; and there wrestled a man with him...

notes.title: Wrestled
notes.body: The verb yeaveq sounds like Jabbok and Jacob, binding name, place, and struggle.

verses.restored: When he saw that he did not prevail against him, he touched the hollow of his thigh, and the hollow of Jacob’s thigh was dislocated as he wrestled with him.

familiar.text: And when he saw that he prevailed not against him, he touched the hollow...

verses.restored: He said, Send me away, for the dawn has risen. He said, I will not send you away unless you bless me.

familiar.text: And he said, Let me go, for the day breaketh...

verses.restored: He said to him, What is your name? He said, Jacob.

familiar.text: And he said unto him, What is thy name? And he said, Jacob.

notes.title: Name confession
notes.body: Jacob must say his name before receiving the new name.

verses.restored: He said, Your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel, for you have struggled with the Divine and with humans, and you have prevailed.

familiar.text: And he said, Thy name shall be called no more Jacob, but Israel...

notes.title: Israel
notes.body: Israel is explained here through struggling with God/the Divine and humans and prevailing.

verses.restored: Jacob asked and said, Please tell me your name. He said, Why is this that you ask my name? And he blessed him there.

familiar.text: And Jacob asked him, and said, Tell me, I pray thee, thy name...

verses.restored: Jacob called the name of the place Peniel, for I have seen the Divine face to face, and my life has been rescued.

familiar.text: And Jacob called the name of the place Peniel: for I have seen God face to face...

notes.title: Peniel
notes.body: Peniel means face of God/the Divine. The face theme continues from Jacob’s fear of Esau’s face.

verses.restored: The sun rose upon him as he passed Penuel, and he was limping because of his thigh.

familiar.text: And as he passed over Penuel the sun rose upon him, and he halted upon his thigh.

verses.restored: Therefore the sons of Israel do not eat the sinew of the hip that is on the hollow of the thigh to this day, because he touched the hollow of Jacob’s thigh at the sinew of the hip.

familiar.text: Therefore the children of Israel eat not of the sinew which shrank...
